Notes: Top level composite state encloses both spa subsystems.
JacuzziController region represents the Jacuzzi subsystem.
Initial transition in JacuzziController goes to JacuzziOff.
JacuzziOff represents the off state.
JacuzziOn is a composite state with pressure pattern and jet operation.
Off to On transitions are provided for central and panel turn on.
On to Off transitions are provided for central and panel turn off.
PressureControl initially enters Level1.
Level1 state is present and sets low pressure.
Increase pressure moves Level1 to Level2 from either control source.
Level2 state is present and sets medium pressure.
Increase pressure moves Level2 to Level3 from either control source.
Decrease pressure moves Level2 to Level1 from either control source.
Level3 state is present and sets high pressure.
Decrease pressure moves Level3 to Level2 from either control source.
No explicit history node is used but parallel regions preserve pressure and pattern while jets pause.
Pattern selection is modeled as valid central and panel self transitions over pattern IDs 1 through 25.
JetsPaused represents the pause feature.
Pause events move running jets to JetsPaused.
After two minutes jets resume while pressure and pattern settings are retained.
SaunaController region represents the sauna subsystem.
Initial transition in SaunaController goes to SaunaOff.
SaunaOff represents the off state.
SaunaOn is a composite state with temperature humidity and water behavior.
SaunaOff transitions to SaunaOn on central turn on.
SaunaOn transitions to SaunaOff on central turn off.
TemperatureControl region represents the heater behavior.
TemperatureControl initially enters TemperatureHeating.
TemperatureHeating represents active stove heating.
Heating transitions to idle when temperature reaches at least 90C.
The transition condition temperatureAtOrAbove90C captures temp >= 90C.
TemperatureIdle represents stove not heating while monitoring temperature.
Idle transitions back to heating when temperature is below 85C.
The transition condition temperatureBelow85C captures temp < 85C.
HumidityControl region represents fan activation based on humidity.
HumidityControl initially starts in a non fan active safe state.
HumiditySafe and HumidityAboveLimitTiming together represent fan off behavior.
Fan activation is modeled after humidity remains above 40 percent for three minutes.
The after3min transition requires humidityPercent > 40.
WaterDispersal region represents eucalyptus water control.
WaterDispersal initially enters WaterReady as the idle waiting state.
WaterReady represents idle readiness for a dispersal request.
Request handling disperses water then enters cooldown and returns to ready which captures the self cycle behavior.
Humidity and fan guards are explicit and the 15 minute limit is enforced by cooldown.
Extra states such as cooldown and timing refine required behavior without contradicting it.
Extra rejection and panel transitions are consistent with the described controls.
Extra guards only enforce valid humidity fan and timing conditions.
Actions describe starting stopping dispensing and timers without adding incorrect behavior.
Additional composite structure separates concurrent concerns appropriately.
Additional regions divide independent control aspects and are behaviorally reasonable.
No incorrect extra history states are present.
